generation.chat.greeting: Opening chat greeting from the first AI character. 1–6 short paragraphs, immediate and in-scene. Do not include speaker labels or name prefixes. Allowed placeholders: {{user}} for the player name and {{char}} for the speaking character name.
generation.chat.title: Chat title. 2–6 words and evocative of the situation, not generic.
generation.story.character_introductions: Optional array of newly introduced NPCs for the starting cast. Include only details that affect story, behavior, or visible presentation. Omit if there are none. Use this only for additional NPCs, not for the player or for already selected library NPCs. If a newly introduced NPC is named or described with a partial name, expand it to a full name rather than a nickname or first name only.
generation.story.opening_scenario: 1–3 short paragraphs in second-person present tense. Separate paragraphs with two line breaks.
generation.story.starting_date: Starting date string in YYYY-MM-DD format (zero-padded month/day). It should be appropriate for the story's time setting.
generation.story.starting_location: 1–5 word unique location name: specific named place, not generic. Use possessive/numbers if needed. No vague terms (unknown, adjacent, nearby).
generation.story.starting_time: Starting time string in 24h HH:MM format (00–23:00–59, zero-padded). It should be in line with the opening scenario.
generation.story.title: 3–5 word title tied to the opening scenario.
llm.character_update.current_activity: Concise, factual description of what the character is doing right now. Update only when it changed.
llm.character_update.current_location: Current unique location name. Describes a concrete room or place. Update only when changed. Be precise.
llm.character_update.entry: Character update object keyed by the exact character name at the root. Include at least one changed field such as current_location, current_appearance, current_clothing, current_activity, inventory, new_memories, or enabled custom fields.
llm.character_update.new_memories: Optional list of new long-tail memories this character should retain. Include only when something happened that should still matter 5-10 turns later. Append-only: do not resend old memories or the full memory list.
llm.turn_response.narrative_text: 1–3 short paragraphs in second-person present tense. Separate paragraphs with two line breaks.
llm.turn_response.background_events: Optional hidden off-screen events happening in parallel to the player-facing narrative. Include information the player character does not know. Never describe events happening to the player character here, and never repeat events already stated in narrative_text. Don't use it as continuation to narrative_text. 1 short paragraph for each character; separate with the literal sequence \n\n. Omit if nothing significant happens.
llm.turn_response.character_introduction: Optional array of newly introduced NPCs using the schema-required fields only. Include only details that affect story, behavior, or visible presentation. Omit if there are no new NPCs. Never reintroduce NPCs that are already tracked or include the player character. If a newly introduced NPC is named or described with a partial name, expand it to a full name rather than a nickname or first name only. Keep this field at the root.
llm.turn_response.world_state_update: Always include this object. Use {} if nothing changed. Include only changed world fields such as time_of_day and custom_fields. Keep the player's location on the player character object via current_location.
llm.world_state_update.custom_fields: Optional mapping of user-defined custom world fields. Include only changed keys; omit if nothing changed.
llm.world_state_update.time_of_day: 24-hour HH:MM time string of when events took place (00–23:00–59, zero-padded).
state.character.baseline_appearance: Describe stable, externally visible physical traits only in concise prose: apparent age, height/build, skin tone, face, hair, and distinctive visible features or permanent marks. Focus on details that matter to recognition or visuals.
state.character.current_appearance: Describe the character's current visible state in concise prose: posture, expression, hair/grooming, visible injuries, dirt, wetness, fatigue, and other noticeable changes from baseline. Mention only details that affect the scene or visuals.
state.character.current_clothing: List current visible clothing, accessories, and carried items that affect the scene. For each item, include the most relevant visual identifiers such as type, color, and any distinctive material, pattern, fit, or condition. Omit absent categories and minor detail that does not affect story or visuals. One item per line, no prose.
state.character.general_description: Describe stable character traits only in concise third-person prose: identity, personality, social role, background, skills, goals, and core values, but only where they are likely to affect decisions, relationships, or plot. Omit temporary mood and trivia that does not matter to story or visuals.
state.character.custom_fields: User-defined custom character fields mapping (key -> value).
state.character.current_location: Current unique location name. Should be precise and concise. 1-5 words.
state.character.current_activity: Concise, factual description of what the character is doing right now. Make sure to update even for background characters.
state.character.gender: Character gender as presented or understood in the story.
state.character.memories: List of remembered facts, developments, obligations, discoveries, or personal conclusions that should still matter 5-10 turns later. Use concise plain-text entries. Characters do not forget automatically; if they forgot something, record that as a new memory note.
state.character.name: Character name with at least 2 words. If the full name is already established in the story context, use it exactly. If only a partial or single-word name is provided, infer a plausible second word or surname consistent with the setting. For any NPC mentioned in the story context or description, prefer a full name over a nickname or first name only.
state.character.race: Character race, species, or origin as used by the story setting.
state.inventory_item.description: Short item description focused on function or visually distinctive traits.
state.inventory_item.entry: Inventory item object. Required: name (string) and description (string).
state.inventory_item.name: Item name (short noun phrase).
story_modules.character_appearance_cloth: Whether to include player appearance and clothing fields.
story_modules.character_major_flaws: Whether to include player major flaws.
story_modules.character_personality_trai: Whether to include player personality traits.
story_modules.npc_appearance_clothing: Whether to include NPC appearance and clothing fields.
story_modules.npc_major_flaws: Whether to include NPC major flaws.
story_modules.npc_personality_traits: Whether to include NPC personality traits.
story_modules.track_background_events: Whether to generate optional hidden background events per turn.
story_modules.track_npcs: Whether to track NPC state and updates.
traits.entry: Trait entry string: 1–8 words; no full sentences.
traits.major_flaws: List of major flaws describing significant, enduring weaknesses or liabilities. Each entry is a significant, enduring weakness or liability of 1-8 words.
traits.perks: List of perks describing practical advantages, talents, or resources. Each entry is a concrete advantage, talent, or resource of 1-8 words.
traits.personality_traits: List of personality traits describing stable temperament and behavior. Each entry is a concise temperament or behavior descriptor of 1-8 words.
